seoTitle: How to Create Tables for Free in Google Docs
metaDescription: Learn how to insert and customize tables in Google Docs at no cost, from choosing grid dimensions to merging cells and adjusting borders.
intro: You don't need paid software to organize data into a clean table inside a document. Google Docs includes a free, built-in table tool with a visual grid selector and a customization sidebar for borders, padding, and cell colors. This guide walks through inserting a table, editing its structure, and styling it to look professional.

steps.heading: Insert a table
steps.body: Place your cursor where you want the table, then go to Insert > Table and drag across the grid selector to choose your rows and columns.

steps.heading: Fill in your data
steps.body: Click into each cell to type your content, using Tab to move between cells.

steps.heading: Add or remove rows and columns
steps.body: Right-click inside a cell and choose options like 'Insert row below' or 'Insert column right' to adjust the table's size.

steps.heading: Merge cells for headers
steps.body: Highlight multiple cells, right-click, and select 'Merge cells' to create a clean header row.

steps.heading: Style with Table Properties
steps.body: Go to Format > Table > Table Properties to adjust background colors, cell padding, and border thickness for a polished finish.

faq.question: Is there any cost to using tables in Google Docs?
faq.answer: No, table insertion and formatting tools are completely free and included in every Google account.

faq.question: Can I resize columns after creating the table?
faq.answer: Yes, you can drag the column border handles directly to adjust individual column widths.

faq.question: How do I make my table fit on one page?
faq.answer: Adjust column widths and cell padding, and consider using a smaller font size if the table spans multiple pages unintentionally.

faq.question: Can I copy a table from Google Sheets into Docs instead?
faq.answer: Yes, you can paste a Sheets table into a Doc, and it will link back to the spreadsheet if you choose that paste option.
